pullRequest.title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Block or normalize `None` resolved-import snapshots during creation.**

This method currently persists `None` directly for new versions. That can create versions that are immediately non-launchable under the new invariant.



<details>
<summary>Proposed hardening</summary>

```diff
+    if (resolvedImportedGitRepos.isEmpty || resolvedImportedProjects.isEmpty) {
+      throw new IllegalArgumentException(
+        "resolvedImportedGitRepos and resolvedImportedProjects must be defined for new app versions; use Some(Seq.empty) when no imports exist"
+      )
+    }
+
     val appVersion = AppVersion(
@@
-      resolvedImportedGitRepos = resolvedImportedGitRepos,
-      resolvedImportedProjects = resolvedImportedProjects,
+      resolvedImportedGitRepos = resolvedImportedGitRepos,
+      resolvedImportedProjects = resolvedImportedProjects,
     )
```
</details>


Also applies to: 133-134

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/implementation/src/main/scala/domino/apps/implementation/DefaultAppVersionService.scala`
around lines 78 - 79, When persisting a new app version in
DefaultAppVersionService, do not allow resolvedImportedGitRepos or
resolvedImportedProjects to be stored as None; instead normalize them to an
explicit empty sequence (e.g. Some(Seq.empty)) or reject creation with a clear
validation error. Update the code path that constructs/persists the new version
(the creation method in DefaultAppVersionService that assigns
resolvedImportedGitRepos and resolvedImportedProjects—also referenced at the
other occurrence around lines 133-134) to replace None with Some(Seq.empty) (or
throw a validation exception) before saving so newly created versions satisfy
the invariant.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**`createAppVersion` contract currently permits writing legacy-marked versions.**

Keeping these parameters optional at the API boundary allows callers to persist `None` for brand-new versions, which can later fail launch checks. Prefer enforcing non-optional inputs (or explicit validation) at creation time.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@apps/interface/src/main/scala/domino/apps/services/AppVersionService.scala`
around lines 71 - 72, The createAppVersion API currently accepts optional
resolvedImportedGitRepos and resolvedImportedProjects which lets callers persist
legacy/None values; update the createAppVersion contract to require non-optional
inputs (change resolvedImportedGitRepos: Option[Seq[ImportedGitRepoSnapshot]]
and resolvedImportedProjects: Option[Seq[ImportedProjectSnapshot]] to non-Option
Seq types) or add explicit validation inside createAppVersion to reject/create
only when those fields are present and non-empty; adjust the createAppVersion
method (and any callers/constructors that build its params) so that either the
parameters are non-optional or throw/return a validation error when they are
None to prevent writing legacy-marked versions.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Confirmed as addressed by @adrianrsy

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->
Main relevant caller sets this value already.

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:
```
#!/bin/bash
# Find all call sites of createAppVersion to verify the claim
rg -n "createAppVersion" --type scala -A 5 -B 2
```

Length of output: 56367

---



</details>

`@adrianrsy`, confirmed — `ModelProductManager.start` (the primary production path) always resolves both values via `commitResolver` before calling `createAppVersion`, so it consistently passes `Some(...)` rather than `None`.

The one other non-test caller worth noting is `ProjectForkerAndCopier` (Line 876), which explicitly passes `None, None` — meaning forked/copied app versions would be flagged as legacy-marked and fail `launchVersion`. If that's intentional (i.e., forked apps need to be re-published to get resolved snapshots), the current design is fine. If not, that path would need its own resolution logic.

Assuming the `ProjectForkerAndCopier` case is a known/accepted limitation, the comment is addressed.

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

**Fix the empty-import Scaladoc contract.**

The doc says `None` means "no imported git repos", but the runtime contract treats `None` as "this version predates snapshotting" and only `Some(Seq.empty)` is valid for fresh versions. Leaving the comment as-is makes future implementations likely to create unlaunchable versions.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@apps/interface/src/main/scala/domino/apps/services/CommitResolver.scala`
around lines 36 - 44, Update the Scaladoc for resolveImportedGitRepos to reflect
the runtime contract: state that returning None means the project version
predates snapshotting, whereas Some(Seq.empty) specifically indicates a fresh
version with no imported git repositories; mention the function signature
(resolveImportedGitRepos(principal: Principal, projectId: DominoId)(implicit
context: DominoContext): Option[Seq[ImportedGitRepoSnapshot]]) and ensure
callers/readers know to treat None as "no snapshotting metadata" and Some(empty)
as "no imported repos".
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Confirmed as addressed by @adrianrsy

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->
Done

`@adrianrsy`, confirmed — the updated Scaladoc in the file now correctly documents the distinction between `None` (version predates snapshotting) and `Some(Seq.empty)` (fresh version with no imported git repos). The contract is clearly stated for future implementors. Thanks!

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Don't silently omit broken imported-project dependencies.**

This `flatMap` drops deleted or archived dependencies and still returns a "successful" snapshot. The resulting `AppVersion` can launch without one of its declared imports, which breaks the reproducibility goal and is hard to diagnose.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@apps/monolith-adapter/src/main/scala/domino/apps/services/DefaultCommitResolver.scala`
around lines 95 - 121, The current dependencies.flatMap silently omits
missing/archived imported projects which yields a misleading successful
snapshot; update the resolution so any dependency that cannot be resolved
(projectsFinder.findById returns empty) or is archived causes a failure (throw
IllegalStateException) instead of being dropped, preserving the existing throws
for missing release/run; to implement, replace the flatMap drop-path with
explicit validation (e.g., use map/for-comprehension or traverse) that checks
projectsFinder.findById, depProject.isArchived, and the release/run lookups and
throws with clear messages that include dependency.projectDependencyId and
dependency.directoryName before constructing ImportedProjectSnapshot (symbols:
dependencies.flatMap / projectsFinder.findById / depProject.isArchived /
ImportedProjectSnapshot / projectReleaseService.findByReleaseId /
runPersister.findById).
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🔴 Critical_

**Avoid creating copied app versions with `None` resolved-import snapshots.**

Line 895 and Line 896 pass `None` for fields that now gate launchability. This can create copied app versions that fail to start immediately.



<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-            resolvedImportedGitRepos = None,
-            resolvedImportedProjects = None,
+            resolvedImportedGitRepos = Some(Seq.empty),
+            resolvedImportedProjects = Some(Seq.empty),
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
            resolvedImportedGitRepos = Some(Seq.empty),
            resolvedImportedProjects = Some(Seq.empty),
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In
`@projects/monolith-adapter/src/main/scala/domino/projects/monoadapter/ProjectForkerAndCopier.scala`
around lines 895 - 896, The copied app-version is being created with
resolvedImportedGitRepos = None and resolvedImportedProjects = None which
prevents the copy from being launchable; update the creation in
ProjectForkerAndCopier so these fields are populated from the source version
(e.g., use the original appVersion.resolvedImportedGitRepos and
appVersion.resolvedImportedProjects or the resolver result used earlier) instead
of None, or wrap the resolved collections in Some(...) (or derive them via the
existing resolveImported... methods) so the new copy carries the same
resolved-import snapshots required for launchability.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

question: If I read this right, setting these to None means any new app version will immediately fail.
Do we need to keep the distinction `None = legacy` X `Some(empty) = "no repos"` here?
Or make these `Some(Seq.empty)`, to be safe, and let the copier resolve it later.

This app version created by ProjectForkerAndCopier is already non-fuctional due to the main commit fields being set to None.
Considering the migration which should handle the majority of legacy cases, None represents "we don't have reproducibility information so we won't allow this to be reproduced".
note: in a rare case where the branch/repo has no commits, this would return null. If easy, we should probably try to handle this, just in case this method gets used for other more general purpose

suggestion: Maybe log a warning when the project is archived? 
It's probably impossible to happen in the context of apps but then, all the more reason for some logging. Especially if we use this for other reasons
question: These methods also served as the fallback for `startVersion` on older persisted versions that only have `commitId` set. I assume we're relying on the migration to account for this case?
Yes, the migration should set these fields for legacy apps.
a lot of this feels like the wrong place, talking about RunStarter and ProjectDependencyGetter in a docstring in the apps/interface directory
Moving the edge case description to ImportedProjectSnapshot model instead and simplifying this scaladoc.
does this break the spirit of this feature? should we fail if an imported project is archived? or is this OK?
Should be an impossible case, but will switch to throwing instead of skipping silently.
nit, hard to read -- else would typically be on the previous line
what is a `release`? is this better/more correct than just do the `filesService.getHeadCommitIdForProject()` below? this feels weird that we're getting the resolved commit from a `run`

Similar to a tag in git if I'm not mistaken but explicitly tied to a run. This should be the correct way to get the commit if the imported project settings specify a release rather than head or a commit.

still feels weird -- why is a "run" the source of truth for a release?

but i'll trust that this is the right way
question: I assume we'll rely on migration to always have `resolvedCommitId` for existing apps?
Yes.
nice documentation!

my other comment about the docs was more high level. in general i feel like docstrings on classes or methods in a `/PROJECT/INTERFACE` kind of directory -- like `/apps/interface` -- should not talk about low level implementation that depends on _other_ projects. `/apps/interface` technically has _no idea_ about runs etc. It's only the monolith-adapter that knows about things like `ExecutionSpecificationBuilder` etc.

so im not convinced this is the best place for these docs, but im fine with it if you can't think of a better place that is closer to the point in the code where all this stuff comes together.
and i really do mean im fine with it! i just wanted to make the more general point clearer

Closer to the execution layer of things would be nice, but the edge cases are only relevant to apps for now and I'm not sure we have a good enough reproducibility story for dependencies across all execution types to make any comments generic to all of them.

I think I'll leave it in here for now.
